title: Anemone × gayeri
intro: Anemone × gayeri, also known as Gayer's anemone, is a hybrid plant species belonging to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in medicine, offering several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in wound healing and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Anemone × gayeri include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Anemone × gayeri is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, including respiratory problems and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Anemone × gayeri is a hybrid species within the genus Anemone. This plant is a cross between Anemone coronaria and Anemone pavoninum. The classification of Anemone × gayeri typically follows a botanical naming convention, indicating its hybrid nature. In this system, the '×' symbol denotes the interspecific hybridization between two different species. As such, Anemone × gayeri is not considered a separate species in the classical sense but rather a cultivated hybrid.
